summary: live viewers reporting micro-freeze + captions lag of ~12s. trade-off: some viewers get smooth video, others hang. @video-team any quick visibility? :rotating_light:
text:
all-hands

jen: live viewers reporting micro-freeze + captions lag of ~12s. trade-off: some viewers get smooth video, others hang. @video-team any quick visibility? :rotating_light:
mark: seeing small burst of UDP retransmits to the ingest cluster around 10:05:42. pulling a short tail now.
mark: ```ingest-frontend WARN retransmits=120 interval=30s
ingest-frontend ERROR session-packet-drop threshold_exceeded```
priya: can we temporarily reduce outgoing bitrate on the encoder to smooth CDN edges while we debug? that would be less disruptive than full failover.
gabe: we can lower to the 4.5Mbps profile for the next 60s. rolling a config now. will warn 15s before change.
ops-bot: Incident created IN-2026-421: all-hands playback microbuffering -> paging video-team.
emma: starting a local capture as precaution: `obs-cli --startrecording --profile=allhands_emergency` writing to /tmp/allhands_emergency_2023456789.mkv
zach: CDN tail shows small region spike in edge jitter around ap-southeast and eu-west. packager is OK; issue likely between ingest and packager workers.
leo: leadership wants to keep Q&A live. recommend adding a slide: "Minor playback issues for some viewers — continuing audio Q&A" and flagging product for a short note.
jen: putting that slide up now and notifying participants.
mark: reducing encoder bitrate to 4.5Mbps in 10...9...8... applying now.
mark: bitrate lowered, monitoring retransmits. packet-loss on ingest dropping. users-report: some say video improved. :eyes:
priya: good call. can we also spin up an alternate low-latency HLS variant for mobile viewers? if we can broadcast a 2.5Mbps LL variant it might help congested routes.
gabe: provisioning LL variant now; will publish manifest tweak in ~45s and CDN purge small manifest.
ops-bot: Action created: TICKET-ALLHANDS-301 "Provision low-latency variant during incident" assigned to video-team.
emma: local capture confirmed writing; starting incremental upload to shared bucket so leo can stitch faster if needed.
mark: logs uploaded to s3://infra-logs/allhands/2023456789/ingest.log (video-team perms).
zach: caption lag persisted a bit — looks like one captions-worker hit a backpressure stall and queued ~9s of frames. restarting worker reduced backlog.
marta: product question: could we add a short banner in the VOD telling users the official recording will be posted after stitch? folks are confused when clips show gaps.
leo: i'll prepare a 30s highlight stitch if we end up with any missing segments; can stitch emma's local + cam SDs. ask stage ops to hold SDs until we confirm archive integrity.
priya: follow-up action: add a preflight check for ingest path P95 retransmits prior to show start (fail if above threshold) and enable automatic local-capture on hosts.
ops-bot: Action logged: TICKET-ALLHANDS-302 "Preflight ingest retransmit check + auto-local-capture" assigned to ops.
gabe: LL variant live; CDN purge completed. early metrics show reduced rebuffer events on mobile traces.
mark: restoring encoder bitrate to normal in 30s as retransmit rates improved. restoring now.
mark: bitrate back to 8Mbps, monitoring. no new errors in the last 90s.
emma: uploading local capture parts to https://storage.redwood.ai/allhands/2023456789/allhands_local_part.mp4 (in-progress).
leo: if we need to stitch anything I'll grab emma files; stage ops confirmed they retained SD for 30m.
jen: thank you — good rapid mitigation. please include the ingest retransmit check and auto-local-capture in the postmortem. i'll compile follow-ups and share the final recording after stitching if needed.
ops-bot: Incident IN-2026-421 marked as mitigated; follow-ups created.
jen: closing the thread after final VOD post. thanks everyone. :white_check_mark:

Ravi (Design): noted. deliverables?
Nina (Marketing): hero static (1200x628), webinar frame (1920x1080, 140px title-safe), gif 1:1 (1080x1080, loop ~3s), slide template (16:9).
Maya (Creative Ops): add: figma source, export tokens, EN/FR copy placeholders. tag legal for any numeric claims.
ExportBot: checklist: PNG @2x, SVG icons, GIF optimized. contrast >=4.5:1.
Ravi (Design): style: data-led (sparklines + metric pills) with small illustrative accent. will mock by Tue EOD, final by Mon AM.
Jon (Growth): include two CTAs for A/B: "See the numbers" and "Register now". also need animated CTA pulse for gif.

Ravi (Design): uploading WIP to Figma: https://www.figma.com/file/EXAMPLE/bench-narrative-pack — frames named "hero-v1" and "webinar-v1". please review.

automated note: "Infra optimization target = 12% run-rate reduction in 90 days via batching, spot, caching, quantization pilots."
Asha (FinanceOps): please file procurement requests via https://redwood.internal/procure and include vendor, term, estimated ARR impact, GL code. Incomplete requests returned.
Diego (Eng): is there an expedited path for customer-required on-prem hardware?

Liam
text:
all-hands

Elena (CFO): Quick thread to follow up on the finance section of today's all-hands — putting answers and actions in one place.
Diego (Eng): thanks 	 can you confirm runway? different numbers floating.
Elena (CFO): base-case runway ~16 months with current signed business. downside stress case (slower ARR) ~9-11 months.
Liam (Sales): does that assume any conservative churn baked in?
Elena (CFO): yes, we assume 5% uplift in churn and conservative sales conversion in Q3-Q4.
Marta (Hiring): should we stop extending offers?
Elena (CFO): not an across-the-board freeze. New rule: only roles approved as "mission-critical" by the hiring committee proceed; others paused and reviewed weekly.
Diego (Eng): procurement thresholds for infra?
Asha (FinanceOps): effective immediately: purchases < $25k = budget owner signoff; $25k-$150k = owner + finance manager; > $150k = owner + finance director + CFO review + 1-page ROI for capital impact.
Diego (Eng): what about multi-year reserved GPU prepaids (3yr)?
Elena (CFO): treated as > $150k. Must include utilization plan and fallback (cancel/transfer options) in ROI doc.
Nina (Product): experiments that raise burn but de-risk a major OKR 	6 weeks 	 still greenlit?
Sam (CEO): yes for a small set. We will fund OKR-aligned bets with clear 6-8 week milestones and measurable success criteria.
Diego (Eng): any concrete infra cost targets?
SpendBot: automated note: "Infra optimization target = 12% run-rate reduction in 90 days via batching, spot, caching, quantization pilots."
Asha (FinanceOps): please file procurement requests via https://redwood.internal/procure and include vendor, term, estimated ARR impact, GL code. Incomplete requests returned.
Diego (Eng): is there an expedited path for customer-required on-prem hardware?
Elena (CFO): mark as "customer-critical" and cc Sales + Solutions + FinanceOps; we'll triage within 48 hours.
Liam (Sales): pricing/discount changes for Dedicated?
Sam (CEO): no price hike; discount bands tightened for new deals. Sales ops will publish updated playbook this week.
Marta (Hiring): headcount forecasting cadence?
Asha (FinanceOps): still quarterly, plus mid-quarter checkpoints if spend reaches 70% of forecast.
Diego (Eng): travel/expense policy changes?
Elena (CFO): capped travel budgets per role band for next two quarters; conference travel needs manager+director signoff; vendor-sponsored travel pre-cleared with FinanceOps.
Nina (Product): will Product get per-route cost/latency visibility to make tradeoffs?
Sam (CEO): yes 	 Console will show per-route token cost and latency next sprint; product teams can request early access.
Asha (FinanceOps): action items: 1) FAQ posted in #finance by EOD tomorrow; 2) procurement form + approval matrix updated; 3) hiring committee publishes prioritization rubric; 4) weekly Finance office hours for infra budget syncs.
Elena (CFO): drop follow-ups here or DM Asha/ me for sensitive items. If you need an accelerated review for a customer-critical ask, @elena or @asha. Thanks all. :thumbsup:
Diego (Eng): thanks 	 helpful.
Marta (Hiring): we'll pause non-critical offers and notify candidates.
SpendBot: snapshot: "Cash runway base 16 months; stress scenario ~10 months if growth decelerates 30%."
